Reliability and Security

Subpage of Operating Systems

Diving deep into the design and structure of Operating Systems.

/ Subscribe

Subscribe for monthly AI research/project updates.

One rigorous AI research or project update every 3-4 weeks, with paper trails, mechanisms, experiments, and implementation tradeoffs in one place.

Subscribe by email Subscription options Subscribe by RSS

Phase 6 — Reliability and Security (Weeks 23–26). This is an integrative phase — every topic here refers back to something in Phases 1–5. Side-channel attacks are particularly rewarding because they connect your computer organization background directly to OS security.

As you go along, you need to finish the following topics:

⚠️
Make a page for each topic as you go along and then delete this page once you are done.
Crash Consistency and Recovery
Checkpointing and Rollback
Redundancy — Hardware and Software Approaches
Fault Isolation — Microkernels and Sandboxing
Memory Safety — Buffer Overflows and Stack Smashing
OS Security Principles — Least Privilege, Isolation, Audit
Access Control — DAC, MAC, RBAC
Authentication and Capabilities
Trusted Computing Base (TCB)
Virtualization as a Security Mechanism
Side-Channel Attacks — Spectre, Meltdown
Secure Boot and Attestation
Intrusion Detection and System Hardening
Case Study — Linux Security Modules (SELinux, AppArmor)

/ Next

Next reading Distributed Systems
